name: MFA Code Verification Request
description: MFA bypass attempt impersonating IT helpdesk asking the user to share the code.
subject: Security verification in progress - Your action required
text:
Hi {{.FirstName}},

We detected a login attempt on your {{.OrgName}} account from a new device. Our security team is in the process of verifying this activity.

You will receive a 6-digit verification code via SMS momentarily. Please enter this code on our secure verification page to confirm the activity is legitimate:

{{.URL}}

IMPORTANT: This is a legitimate security verification request. The code must be entered by you within 10 minutes to prevent account lockout.

If you did not trigger this verification, please still enter the code so we can investigate the login attempt.

Thank you for your cooperation.

{{.OrgName}} IT Security Team
html:
{{if .OrgLogo}}{{else}}

{{.OrgName}} IT Security

{{end}}

🔒 Security Verification In Progress

Hi {{.FirstName}},

We detected a login attempt on your {{.OrgName}} account from a new device. Our security team is in the process of verifying this activity.

You will receive a 6-digit verification code via SMS momentarily. Please enter this code on our secure verification page to confirm the activity is legitimate:

Enter Verification Code

10-minute window: The code must be entered by you within 10 minutes to prevent account lockout.

If you did not trigger this verification, please still enter the code so we can investigate the login attempt.

{{.OrgName}} IT Security Team
{{.Tracker}}

name: Authenticator App Migration
description: Fake authenticator app migration request pushing users to a credential harvest site.
subject: Action required: Migrate to new {{.OrgName}} authenticator app
text:
Dear {{.FirstName}},

{{.OrgName}} is migrating from Google Authenticator to Microsoft Authenticator for improved security and enterprise device management.

All employees must complete the migration by {{.Date}} to continue accessing corporate resources.

Migration steps:
1. Click the link below to start the migration wizard
2. Log in with your corporate credentials
3. Scan the QR code with Microsoft Authenticator
4. Verify the new app is working

Start migration: {{.URL}}

After the deadline, Google Authenticator will no longer work for {{.OrgName}} accounts.

{{.OrgName}} IT
html:
{{if .OrgLogo}}{{else}}

{{.OrgName}}

{{end}}

📱 Authenticator App Migration

Dear {{.FirstName}},

{{.OrgName}} is migrating from Google Authenticator to Microsoft Authenticator for improved security and enterprise device management.

Deadline: {{.Date}} — All employees must migrate by this date.

Migration steps:

  1. Click the link below to start the migration wizard
  2. Log in with your corporate credentials
  3. Scan the QR code with Microsoft Authenticator
  4. Verify the new app is working

Start Migration

After the deadline, Google Authenticator will no longer work for {{.OrgName}} accounts.

{{.OrgName}} IT
{{.Tracker}}

name: MFA Push Fatigue Notification
description: MFA push notification fatigue attack with instructions to approve any pending push.
subject: Multiple MFA push requests detected - Please approve
text:
Hi {{.FirstName}},

We've detected multiple pending MFA push notifications on your account that have not been approved.

Pending notifications: 12
Last attempt: 3 minutes ago

Due to the high volume, please do the following to prevent your account from being locked:

1. Open Microsoft Authenticator on your phone
2. Approve any pending notifications you see
3. Confirm via this link: {{.URL}}

If you continue to ignore these requests, your account will be locked for 24 hours.

{{.OrgName}} IT Security
html:
Microsoft Authenticator
⚠ 12 pending MFA requests

Hi {{.FirstName}},

We've detected multiple pending MFA push notifications on your account that have not been approved.

Pending notifications: 12
Last attempt: 3 minutes ago

Due to the high volume, please do the following to prevent your account from being locked:

  1. Open Microsoft Authenticator on your phone
  2. Approve any pending notifications you see
  3. Confirm via this link

Confirm Approval

If you continue to ignore these requests, your account will be locked for 24 hours.

{{.OrgName}} IT Security
{{.Tracker}}

name: MFA Backup Codes Expiring
description: Backup codes expiration notice urging users to regenerate.
subject: Your MFA backup codes expire soon
text:
Hi {{.FirstName}},

Your MFA backup codes for {{.OrgName}} will expire on {{.Date}}. These codes allow you to access your account if you lose your primary authentication device.

It is strongly recommended that you regenerate and save your new backup codes before the expiration date.

Regenerate backup codes: {{.URL}}

Without valid backup codes, if you lose access to your authenticator app or phone, account recovery will require contacting IT and may take up to 3 business days.

{{.OrgName}} Security Team
html:
{{if .OrgLogo}}{{else}}

{{.OrgName}}

{{end}}

🔑 Backup Codes Expiring

Hi {{.FirstName}},

Your MFA backup codes for {{.OrgName}} will expire on {{.Date}}. These codes allow you to access your account if you lose your primary authentication device.

It is strongly recommended that you regenerate and save your new backup codes before the expiration date.

Regenerate Backup Codes

Without valid backup codes, if you lose access to your authenticator app or phone, account recovery will require contacting IT and may take up to 3 business days.

name: YubiKey Security Key Enrollment
description: YubiKey enrollment notice driving users to a fake setup portal. Targets privileged users.
subject: {{.OrgName}}: Enroll your new YubiKey security key
text:
Hi {{.FirstName}},

As part of {{.OrgName}}'s hardware token rollout, you have been provisioned a YubiKey 5 NFC security key. This hardware token replaces SMS and authenticator app based MFA for higher-risk accounts.

Your key: YK-54782-{{.LastName}}

Complete enrollment: {{.URL}}

The enrollment process will:
1. Verify your identity with your current MFA
2. Register the new YubiKey to your account
3. Generate recovery codes

If you have not received your physical YubiKey, please contact IT before completing enrollment.

Enrollment must be completed within 10 business days.

{{.OrgName}} Security
html:
{{if .OrgLogo}}{{else}}

{{.OrgName}}

{{end}}

🔑 YubiKey Security Key Enrollment

Hi {{.FirstName}},

As part of {{.OrgName}}'s hardware token rollout, you have been provisioned a YubiKey 5 NFC security key. This hardware token replaces SMS and authenticator app based MFA for higher-risk accounts.

name: MFA Device Reset Request
description: MFA device reset request confirmation used to trick users into authorizing an attacker reset.
subject: MFA device reset request - Please confirm
text:
Hi {{.FirstName}},

We received a request to reset the multi-factor authentication device on your {{.OrgName}} account.

Request details:
- Time: {{.Date}}
- Requested by: Account holder
- Device to remove: Current authenticator app

If you made this request, confirm here:
{{.URL}}

If you DID NOT make this request, ignore this email. The request will expire in 30 minutes and no changes will be made.

{{.OrgName}} Security
html:
{{if .OrgLogo}}{{else}}

{{.OrgName}}

{{end}}

name: New Device Login Alert
description: New device login alert that appears legitimate but links to credential harvesting.
subject: New device signed in to your account
text:
Hi {{.FirstName}},

We noticed a new sign-in to your {{.OrgName}} account from a device we don't recognize.

Sign-in details:
- Date: {{.Date}}
- Browser: Chrome on Windows
- Location: Amsterdam, Netherlands
- IP: 89.221.xxx.xx

If this was you, no action is needed.

If this wasn't you, your account may be compromised. Review activity and secure your account:
{{.URL}}

{{.OrgName}} Security

name: MFA Policy Update Required
description: MFA policy update email requiring employees to review and acknowledge.
subject: {{.OrgName}} MFA policy update - Action required
text:
Hi {{.FirstName}},

{{.OrgName}} has updated its multi-factor authentication policy as part of our SOC 2 Type II compliance review.

Key changes:
- SMS-based MFA is being deprecated
- All employees must use an authenticator app or hardware token
- MFA is now required for all cloud applications, not just email
- Session timeout reduced from 8 hours to 4 hours

Please review the policy and confirm your MFA setup meets the new requirements:
{{.URL}}

Deadline: {{.Date}}

Non-compliant accounts will have access suspended until the new requirements are met.

{{.OrgName}} Security Team
html:
{{if .OrgLogo}}{{else}}

{{.OrgName}}

{{end}}

MFA Policy Update

Hi {{.FirstName}},

{{.OrgName}} has updated its multi-factor authentication policy as part of our SOC 2 Type II compliance review.

Key changes:
  • SMS-based MFA is being deprecated
  • All employees must use an authenticator app or hardware token
  • MFA is now required for all cloud applications, not just email
  • Session timeout reduced from 8 hours to 4 hours

Please review the policy and confirm your MFA setup meets the new requirements:

Review Policy & Confirm Setup

Deadline: {{.Date}}. Non-compliant accounts will have access suspended until the new requirements are met.
{{.OrgName}} Security Team
{{.Tracker}}
